title: Build a Servo Gripper
description: Cut and hinge two jaws onto a micro servo so your robot can pinch and release small objects safely.

dialogue.text: A gripper is a simple way to interact with the world. We'll build it with Orion's two-path drill: either mock-fit first to catch geometry mistakes, or go straight to assembly if your template is proven.

options.text: Mock-fit first so linkage travel is predictable.
I already validated the template; go to final assembly.

dialogue.text: Dry-fit both jaws and cycle by hand. If the hinge binds before mid-travel, widen the slot and recenter the horn before fastening.

options.text: Travel is smooth and symmetric now.

dialogue.text: Mount the servo vertically and connect both fingers with linkage. Keep wire slack away from the jaw path and set a soft-close limit to avoid stripping gears.

options.text: Run and log five open/close cycles
Cycle log captured with no chatter.

dialogue.text: Interpret the sweep log: no hard stops, no skipped pulses, and repeatable jaw closure. If the jaw stalls or chatters, branch to recovery before trying to finish.

options.text: Verification passed: closure is repeatable and safe.
I saw chatter, binding, or asymmetric closure.

dialogue.text: Recovery path: power down, loosen linkage one turn, re-center neutral pulse, then rerun the cycle log. Do not continue if gear noise persists.

options.text: Recovery complete; rerun the verification log.

dialogue.text: Nice! Your gripper now has a documented safe operating range and can reliably handle small payloads.
